name: Vía Verde de la Demanda — circular desde Pineda de la Sierra
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Pineda De La Sierra traverse the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de la Demanda in Burgos, Spain. The region features a mix of mountains, extensive pine and oak forests, and river valleys. Key physical features include the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda, a converted railway line, and high peaks such as San Millán. The area also encompasses the Arlanzón River and its reservoir, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Pineda De La Sierra The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra de la Demanda Greenway and Arlanzón Reservoir Loop, a 32.5 miles (52.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This route follows the Vía Verde, offering a journey through valleys and past the Arlanzón Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía Verde de la Demanda — circular desde Pineda de la Sierra, a difficult 16.1 miles (26.0 km) path. This circular route provides varied terrain along the Vía Verde with significant elevation gain. Local touring cyclists also love the Pineda de la Sierra – Puerto del Manquillo loop from El Peñedo, a 49.1 miles (79.0 km) trail leading through dense pine forests and over the Puerto del Manquillo mountain pass, often completed in about 5 hours 19 minutes. Touring cycling around Pineda De La Sierra is defined by converted railway lines, mountain passes, and dense woodlands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels. The routes in Pineda De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Pineda De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Pineda de la Sierra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Pineda de la Sierra offer a diverse landscape. You'll encounter a mix of well-maintained gravel sections and smooth tarmac, especially along the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda. The region features valleys, extensive pine and oak forests, and mountainous areas, including challenging ascents on routes like the one passing over &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Manquillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Pineda de la Sierra?
A: There are 19 touring cycling routes available around Pineda de la Sierra. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's an option for almost every cyclist.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easy ride?
A: Yes, Pineda de la Sierra offers 2 easy touring cycling routes. While many routes present a challenge, there are options designed for a more relaxed pace, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027950160/via-verde-de-la-demanda-circular-desde-pineda-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Demanda — circular desde Pineda de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 26.0 km with significant elevation gain. Another is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arlanzón Reservoir – Arlanzón Reservoir loop from Villasur de Herreros&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes showcase the region's natural beauty and historical charm. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5192583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arlanzón Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, through dense pine and oak forests, and even over mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Manquillo&lt;/a&gt;. The Vía Verde itself is a highlight, offering unique views as it follows an old railway line. You might also spot high peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán (2,131 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4002054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mencilla&lt;/a&gt; in the distance.

Q: Is the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda suitable for touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. The Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda is a primary draw for touring cyclists. It's a traffic-free route, approximately 54 kilometers long, following a converted old mining railway line. While it features well-maintained gravel and tarmac sections, be aware that some parts, especially where tunnels are closed, can have steep alternative tracks with gradients up to 26%. It's ideal for gravel bikes and offers an immersive experience through varied landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Pineda de la Sierra?
A: The region is beautiful year-round, but autumn is particularly stunning when the extensive pine, oak, and beech forests burst with color. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and lush greenery. Summers can be warm, while winters may bring snow to higher elevations, impacting route accessibility, especially on mountain passes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Pineda de la Sierra?
A: While many routes in the region can be challenging, there are 2 easy routes that might be more suitable for families. When choosing a route, consider the distance and elevation gain to ensure it matches the fitness level of all family members. The Vía Verde, with its traffic-free nature, can be a good option for sections that are less steep.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Pineda de la Sierra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the tranquility of the extensive forests, and the unique experience of cycling along the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda. The blend of challenging mountain passes and scenic reservoir loops also receives positive feedback.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Pineda de la Sierra?
A: Pineda de la Sierra, being a charming mountain village, offers various parking options. For routes starting directly from the village, you'll find local parking. For access to the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda, there are often designated parking areas at key access points along the route. It's advisable to check specific route details for recommended starting points and parking facilities.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes in Pineda de la Sierra using public transport?
A: Access to Pineda de la Sierra and its cycling routes via public transport can be limited, as it's a more rural area. While there might be bus services connecting to larger towns in the province of Burgos, direct public transport links specifically catering to cyclists with bikes might be infrequent. Planning your journey in advance and checking local bus schedules is recommended.

Q: Are there cafes or restaurants along the touring cycling routes in Pineda de la Sierra?
A: Pineda de la Sierra itself has local establishments where you can find cafes and restaurants. While some longer routes might pass through other small villages with amenities, many sections of the routes, especially through dense forests or along the Vía Verde, are quite remote. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, and plan your stops in advance.
tips: The Burgos slope from Barbadillo del Pez is an 11-kilometre climb with gradients of up to 11%. The narrow, undivided road runs through the mainly pine forest mass. It is an ideal mountain pass for those who want to lose themselves on roads practically abandoned by cars and enjoy cycling in its purest form.
Really a nice photo opportunity. In the village is a nice little café ideal for a break.

It is a spectacular Romanesque church where its majestic portal, dating from the 12th century, and the wonderful arcaded gallery stand out.
You can entertain yourself with all its details, such as the curious carvings, while you rest a bit if you are pedaling in the area.

Church of Saint Stephen the Protomartyr
